name: Hong Kong (British, 1959–1997)

colors.symbolism: details of the Union Flag and the colonial badge
details of the Union Flag and the colonial badge

description: A British Blue Ensign — navy field with the Union Flag in the upper hoist canton — defaced in the fly with the coat of arms of Hong Kong (a Chinese-style sailing junk above blue waves with mountains beyond, beneath a heraldic shield).
history: Flag of British Hong Kong, used from 1959 (when the colonial coat of arms was granted) until the handover to the People's Republic of China on 1 July 1997. Replaced on that date by the bauhinia-flower flag of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region.

features.role: Coat of arms of Hong Kong (Chinese junk on blue waters with mountains)
